title: Macros: The Reflection of Self
overview: Small programs that run during compilation to expand code into more complex structures.
historical_context: Born in Lisp and evolved into powerful hygienic macros in Scheme and Rust.
mental_model: Code that writes code based on structural patterns.
tradeoffs: Difficult to debug, can lead to confusing error messages, and "macro-expansion" complexity.
legacy: Key to Lisp’s longevity and Rust’s expressive power.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: What makes a macro "hygienic" and why does it matter?
Analyze the differences between Lisp’s defmacro and Rust’s proc_macros.
How are macros used to implement features that usually require compiler support?
